message.content:
You are repairing an AsciiDoc procedural sub-spec to align it with sibling files for cross-spec consistency. Pure .adoc work, no code, no commits.

TARGET FILE:
/Users/bhyslop/projects/rbm_alpha_recipemuster/Tools/rbk/vov_veiled/RBSDK-director_invest.adoc

THREE REPAIRS TO LAND IN THIS FILE:

REPAIR A — Convert prose-style opener to NOTE: block (matching RBSDE-depot_levy.adoc:4-13 gold standard).

CURRENT lines 4-11:
```
The
{rbtr_governor}
invests a
{rbtr_director}
{giam_service_account}
with build management permissions.
This operation accepts no instance argument; the service account identity is derived entirely from regime constants as
`${RBCC_role_director}-${RBRR_DEPOT_MONIKER}`.
```

REPLACE WITH (verbatim — do not rephrase):
```
NOTE: This operation invests a {rbtr_director} {giam_service_account} into the {rbtgi_depot_project}.
The {rbtr_governor} mints the SA, generates its credential key, and writes the RBRA bytes to the assay output slot.
The operation is singleton-per-role: no instance argument; the SA identity is derived from regime constants as `${RBCC_role_director}-${RBRR_DEPOT_MONIKER}`.
Fatal if the SA already exists — operator must {rbtgo_director_divest} first to re-key.
```

REPAIR B — Restore `//axhoot_typed_output` block as the credential-output declaration; collapse the current bare `//axhos_step` "Write Credential to Assay Output Slot" into this block (RBSDE gold standard uses //axhoot_typed_output for typed output declarations; orphans declared {rbtgi_rbra_file}).

CURRENT lines 159-162:
```
//axhos_step
. *Write Credential to Assay Output Slot*:
.. {rbbc_store}
`«DIRECTOR_RBRA_FILE»` RBRA bytes to the assay output slot via the producer-output discipline
```

REPLACE WITH (verbatim):
```
//axhoot_typed_output
{rbtgi_rbra_file}
. *Deliver Credential to Assay Output Slot*:
.. {rbbc_store}
`«DIRECTOR_RBRA_FILE»` RBRA bytes to the assay output slot via the producer-output discipline (the orphan's `${BURD_OUTPUT_DIR}` credential write retires)
```

REPAIR C — Verify `//axhoc_completion` prose at the end remains intact; do not modify it.

CONSTRAINTS:
- Do NOT touch any other files. Do NOT make any commits.
- Do NOT modify the 13 //axhos_step blocks in the body — only the opening prose (REPAIR A) and the final credential-output block (REPAIR B).
- The blank line currently at line 12 between opener and //axhos_step should remain as a blank line between the new NOTE: block and the first //axhos_step.

After editing, report in 5 lines max:
1. Final line count
2. Confirmation: opener is now a NOTE: block (4 lines)
3. Confirmation: the file contains exactly one `//axhoot_typed_output` opener
4. The exact line number where `//axhoot_typed_output` appears
5. Any anomaly noticed

content.text: I'll read the target file first, then apply the three repairs.

content.content:
1	//axhob_operation
2	{rbtgo_director_invest}
3	
4	The
5	{rbtr_governor}
6	invests a
7	{rbtr_director}
8	{giam_service_account}
9	with build management permissions.
10	This operation accepts no instance argument; the service account identity is derived entirely from regime constants as
11	`${RBCC_role_director}-${RBRR_DEPOT_MONIKER}`.
12	
13	//axhos_step
14	. *Authenticate as
15	{rbtr_governor}*:
16	.. Execute
17	{rbtoe_governor_authenticate}
18	.. {rbbc_store}
19	`«GOVERNOR_TOKEN»` for subsequent API calls
20	
21	//axhos_step
22	. *Derive
23	{rbtr_director}
24	Service Account Identity*:
25	.. {rbbc_store}
26	`«DIRECTOR_ACCOUNT_NAME»` as `${RBCC_role_director}-${RBRR_DEPOT_MONIKER}` (singleton per role per depot, regime-kindled via
27	{rbrr_depot_moniker})
28	.. {rbbc_store}
29	`«DIRECTOR_EMAIL»` as `«DIRECTOR_ACCOUNT_NAME»@${RBDC_DEPOT_PROJECT_ID}.iam.gserviceaccount.com` (depot project ID derived from `(RBRR_CLOUD_PREFIX, RBRR_DEPOT_MONIKER)`)
30	
31	//axhos_step
32	. *Preflight: Confirm Service Account Does Not Exist*:
33	.. {rbbc_call}
34	link:https://cloud.google.com/iam/docs/reference/rest/v1/projects.serviceAccounts/get[iam.serviceAccounts.get] for `projects/-/serviceAccounts/«DIRECTOR_EMAIL»`
35	.. {rbbc_fatal}
36	if HTTP 200: service account already exists — operator must execute
37	{rbtgo_director_divest}
38	before re-investing this role
39	.. {rbbc_require}
40	HTTP 404 to proceed
41	
42	//axhos_step
43	. *Create
44	{rbtr_director}
45	Service Account*:
46	.. {rbbc_call}
47	link:https://cloud.google.com/iam/docs/reference/rest/v1/projects.serviceAccounts/create[iam.serviceAccounts.create] with:
48	... accountId: `«DIRECTOR_ACCOUNT_NAME»`
49	... displayName: `Recipe Bottle Director`
50	... description: `Create/destroy container images for depot ${RBRR_DEPOT_MONIKER}`
51	... project:
52	{rbtgi_depot_project}
53	.. {rbbc_require}
54	HTTP 200 response
55	.. {rbbc_store}
56	`«DIRECTOR_UNIQUE_ID»` from response `.uniqueId` field
57	
58	//axhos_step
59	. *Verify Service Account Propagation*:
60	.. {rbbc_call}
61	link:https://cloud.google.com/iam/docs/reference/rest/v1/projects.serviceAccounts/get[iam.serviceAccounts.get] for `projects/-/serviceAccounts/«DIRECTOR_EMAIL»`
62	.. {rbbc_poll}
63	with backoff until HTTP 200 (newly-created SA may not be immediately resolvable by email)
64	.. NOTE: Poll must use the email address, not the numeric ID. The email-based lookup path has independent propagation from the UID-based path, and subsequent steps (keys.list, keys.create) all use the email address. Polling by UID can succeed while the email path still returns 404.
65	.. {rbbc_require}
66	service account accessible by email
67	
68	//axhos_step
69	. *Preflight: Ensure Pristine Key State*:
70	.. {rbbc_call}
71	link:https://cloud.google.com/iam/docs/reference/rest/v1/projects.serviceAccounts.keys/list[serviceAccounts.keys.list] for `«DIRECTOR_EMAIL»`
72	.. {rbbc_store}
73	`«EXISTING_KEYS»` from response `.keys[]`
74	.. {rbbc_require}
75	no keys with `keyType: USER_MANAGED` exist
76	.. {rbbc_fatal}
77	if USER_MANAGED keys found: "Manual cleanup required via Console"
78	
79	//axhos_step
80	. *Generate Service Account Key*:
81	.. {rbbc_call}
82	link:https://cloud.google.com/iam/docs/reference/rest/v1/projects.serviceAccounts.keys/create[serviceAccounts.keys.create] for `«DIRECTOR_EMAIL»` with:
83	... privateKeyType: `TYPE_GOOGLE_CREDENTIALS_FILE`
84	.. Retry up to `RBGC_SA_KEY_CREATE_RETRY_MAX` times on HTTP 404 with `RBGC_SA_KEY_CREATE_RETRY_DELAY_SEC` delay between attempts
85	.. NOTE: SA read operations (GET by email, keys.list) may succeed while keys.create POST returns 404 "does not exist" due to write-path propagation delay. Google link:https://cloud.google.com/iam/docs/service-accounts-create[documents]: "you might need to wait 60 seconds or more before you use the service account." The 404 confirms no key was created, making retry safe. See <<rbscig_iam_grant_contracts>>.
86	.. {rbbc_store}
87	`«DIRECTOR_PRIVATE_KEY_DATA»` from response `.privateKeyData` field
88	.. {rbtoe_rbra_generate}
89	converts `«DIRECTOR_PRIVATE_KEY_DATA»` to `«DIRECTOR_RBRA_FILE»`
90	
91	//axhos_step
92	. *Get Depot Project Number*:
93	.. {rbbc_call}
94	link:https://cloud.google.com/resource-manager/reference/rest/v3/projects/get[projects.get] for
95	{rbtgi_depot_project}
96	.. {rbbc_store}
97	`«PROJECT_NUMBER»` from `.projectNumber` field
98	
99	//axhos_step
100	. *Grant Project-Level Permissions*:
101	.. {rbtoe_iam_grant_project} for
102	{rbtgi_depot_project}
103	.. Add `serviceAccount:«DIRECTOR_EMAIL»` with roles:
104	... `roles/cloudbuild.builds.editor` (submit and manage builds)
105	... `roles/viewer` (read project resources)
106	... `roles/cloudbuild.workerPoolUser` (use private pool for vouch `builds.create`)
107	.. Policy version: 3
108	.. {rbbc_require}
109	IAM policy update successful
110	
111	//axhos_step
112	. *Grant
113	{rbtr_mason}
114	Impersonation*:
115	.. NOTE:
116	{rbtr_director}
117	submits builds that execute as
118	{rbtr_mason}.
119	This requires `serviceAccountUser` permission on
120	{rbtr_mason}.
121	.. {rbtoe_iam_grant_sa} on
122	{rbtr_mason}
123	service account
124	.. Add `serviceAccount:«DIRECTOR_EMAIL»` with `roles/iam.serviceAccountUser`
125	.. {rbbc_require}
126	IAM binding successful
127	
128	//axhos_step
129	. *Grant
130	{rbtgi_build_bucket}
131	Access*:
132	.. {rbtoe_iam_grant_bucket} on
133	{rbtgi_build_bucket}
134	.. Add `serviceAccount:«DIRECTOR_EMAIL»` with roles:
135	... `roles/storage.objectCreator` (upload sources)
136	... `roles/storage.objectViewer` (verify uploads)
137	.. {rbbc_require}
138	bucket IAM update successful
139	
140	//axhos_step
141	. *Grant
142	{rbtgi_repository}
143	Admin Access*:
144	.. {rbbc_call}
145	link:https://cloud.google.com/artifact-registry/docs/reference/rest/v1/projects.locations.repositories/getIamPolicy[repositories.getIamPolicy] via HTTP GET with `options.requestedPolicyVersion=3` query parameter
146	.. NOTE: {gar_registry} proto specifies GET for `getIamPolicy` with no `additional_bindings` for POST. Using POST fails intermittently with HTTP 404 (transcoding layer rejection, not missing repository). See <<rbscig_iam_grant_contracts>>.
147	.. {rbbc_require}
148	HTTP 200 — 404 means repository does not exist (real error, not empty policy)
149	.. {rbbc_call}
150	link:https://cloud.google.com/artifact-registry/docs/reference/rest/v1/projects.locations.repositories/setIamPolicy[repositories.setIamPolicy] for
151	{rbtgi_repository}
152	.. *Complete policy write*: Add ALL expected bindings to the read policy, regardless of what the read returned:
153	... `serviceAccount:«DIRECTOR_EMAIL»` with `roles/artifactregistry.repoAdmin` — Director manages images
154	... `serviceAccount:«MASON_EMAIL»` with `roles/artifactregistry.writer` — Mason pushes images at build time
155	.. NOTE: Same complete-policy-write pattern as the secret IAM step. Prevents stale read from overwriting Mason's writer binding set by {rbtgo_depot_levy}.
156	.. {rbbc_require}
157	repository IAM set with all expected bindings
158	
159	//axhos_step
160	. *Write Credential to Assay Output Slot*:
161	.. {rbbc_store}
162	`«DIRECTOR_RBRA_FILE»` RBRA bytes to the assay output slot via the producer-output discipline
163	
164	//axhoc_completion
165	Successful execution invests director service account `«DIRECTOR_ACCOUNT_NAME»` with build management permissions, generates its credential key, and writes the RBRA file to the assay output slot.
166

input.new_string:
NOTE: This operation invests a {rbtr_director} {giam_service_account} into the {rbtgi_depot_project}.
The {rbtr_governor} mints the SA, generates its credential key, and writes the RBRA bytes to the assay output slot.
The operation is singleton-per-role: no instance argument; the SA identity is derived from regime constants as `${RBCC_role_director}-${RBRR_DEPOT_MONIKER}`.
Fatal if the SA already exists — operator must {rbtgo_director_divest} first to re-key.
